channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Elon Musk testifies against OpenAI, seeking Sam Altman's ouster
item.description: Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."
item.content:encoded:
Musk's lawyers say OpenAI leaders "stole a charity" and Musk warns about the potential dangers of AI: "We don't want to have a Terminator outcome."

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC oil cartel after nearly 60 years
item.description: The Arab oil producer has long expressed frustration with the quotas it has to follow as part of OPEC, the cartel of major state-owned oil producers.
item.content:encoded:
The Arab oil producer has long expressed frustration with the quotas it has to follow as part of OPEC, the cartel of major state-owned oil producers.

(Image credit: Joe Klamar)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured. NPR wants to hear your story
item.description: Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.
item.content:encoded:
Millions of homes in the U.S. are uninsured, partly because insurance costs have soared in recent years. NPR wants to hear about the coverage decisions you're making as premiums rise.

(Image credit: ROBYN BECK/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Lawsuits accuse State Farm of secretly working to cut insurance payouts
item.description: Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.
item.content:encoded:
Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel responds to Trump's call for him to be fired over joke about first lady
item.description: Days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner ended in gunfire, Kimmel said Melania Trump had a "glow like an expectant widow." It was not "a call to assassination, and they know that," he said.
item.content:encoded: Days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner ended in gunfire, Kimmel said Melania Trump had a "glow like an expectant widow." It was not "a call to assassination, and they know that," he said.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
item.description: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."
item.content:encoded:
Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Climate disaster victims are rebuilding using prefab homes from boxy to bespoke
item.description: Wildfires, hurricanes, tornadoes and floods fueled by manmade climate change are changing the housing industry. That's because people are embracing prefab homes that can withstand extreme weather.
item.content:encoded:
Wildfires, hurricanes, tornadoes and floods fueled by manmade climate change are changing the housing industry. That's because people are embracing prefab homes that can withstand extreme weather.

(Image credit: Vanessa Romo)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Musk vs. Altman: Tech CEOs head to court over the fate of OpenAI
item.description: The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.
item.content:encoded:
The former OpenAI business partners are embroiled in a high-stakes dispute over the future of one of the world's top AI companies.

(Image credit: Michael Kovac/Getty Images for Vanity Fair)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Ingenious? Orwellian? Or both? Supreme Court considers constitutionality of 'geofence' warrants
item.description: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.
item.content:encoded: The technique allows police to tap into giant tech-firm databases to find out who was near the scene of a crime and may have been involved.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
